data.title: StanChart pushes India rate cut call to April from Feb amid tight liquidity
data.description:
• Standard Chartered Bank has pushed its call for Reserve Bank of India's rate cut to April from February amid tightness in rupee liquidity

  • It now expects 50 bps of rate cut in April and June, against earlier expectation of February and April

  • It also expects the RBI to slash banks' cash reserve ratio one more time by 50 basis points in February monetary policy meeting

  • "Given that a tight liquidity scenario would conflict with the RBI's stated neutral monetary policy stance, we think the central bank is likely to prioritise liquidity enhancing measures rather than a repo rate cut at the February meeting," the foreign bank says in a note

  • RBI shifted to neutral policy stance in Oct and cut banks' CRR by 50 bps in Dec meeting

  • It anticipates headline liquidity deficit to widen to 2-2.5 trillion rupees, with core liquidity switching to a deficit of around 1-1.5 trillion rupees by the end of March 2025

data.title: Buchang Pharma's Unit Gets Approval to Market Oseltamivir Phosphate Dry Suspension
data.description:
Shandong Buchang Pharmaceuticals' unit, Baoding Tianhao Pharmaceutical, received the drug registration certificate for oseltamivir phosphate dry suspension from the Chinese drug administration, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

The certificate will allow the manufacturing and sales of the drug, which is used for the treatment of patients with influenza A and B.

The pharmaceutical company invested 14.4 million yuan into the research and development of the drug.

data.title: Sunac China: Received Winding Up Petition Issued by China Cinda (HK) Asset Management Against Company in Hong Kong
Sunac China: Hearing Scheduled for Mar. 19
Court Approval Received and Record Date Determined for the Chlore Property Spinoff Pursuant to Arrangement
data.description:
VANCOUVER, BC / ACCESSWIRE / January 9, 2025 / Zeus North America Mining Corp. (CSE:ZEUS)(OTCQB:ZUUZF)(FRANKFURT:O92) (the "Company" or "Zeus") reports that on January 7, 2025 the Supreme Court of British Columbia approved the plan of arrangement between the Company and its wholly owned subsidiary Kelso Mining Inc. ("Kelso") dated August 26, 2024 (the "Arrangement"), to spin out its Chlore Property located in the Omineca Mining Division of North-Central British Columbia to Kelso.

The Company announced the share distribution record date of January 29, 2025 (the "Share Distribution Record Date") pursuant to the Arrangement. Under the terms of the Arrangement, Zeus' shareholders will be issued one share of Kelso with respect to every 150 shares of Zeus owned on the Share Distribution Record Date.

The Company plans to complete the Arrangement and distribute Kelso shares to the Company's shareholders and transfer the Chlore Property to Kelso on February 5, 2025.

Holders of Zeus options and warrants, who exercise their options and/or warrants before the Share Distribution Record Date, will also be entitled to receive one share of Kelso with respect to every 150 shares of Zeus. The Chlore Property will be transferred to Kelso.

Upon completion of the Arrangement, Zeus shareholders will ultimately own shares in two public companies: Zeus, which will be focused on its properties in the State of Idaho known as the Cuddy Mountain, Selway and Great Western properties and Kelso, which will focus on the Chlore Property.

The disclosure regarding the Arrangement is provided in the Company's information circular dated November 5, 2024 and the news releases dated August 26, 2024 and December 20, 2024, which are available on www.sedarplus.com under the profile of Zeus North America Mining Corp.

On behalf of the board of directors.

"Dean Besserer"

President and CEO

For more information, please contact the Company at info@zeusminingcorp.com

FOR INVESTOR RELATIONS CONTACT:

Kin Communications Inc.

Ph: 604-684-6730

ZEUS@kincommunications.com

About Zeus North America Mining Corp.

Exploring in Idaho's newest Copper Belt. The Company is focused on its exploration properties in the state of Idaho, USA known as the: Cuddy Mountain; Selway; and Great Western properties, respectively. The Idaho properties consist of 102 (Cuddy Mountain), 57 (Selway) and 38 (Great Western) lode mining claims respectively and cover a cumulative area of approximately 4,200 acres. The Company's flagship Cuddy Mountain Property is adjacent to Hercules Metal Corp.'s Leviathan Copper Porphyry discovery. The Company is in the business of mineral exploration.

Forward-Looking Statements

When used in this news release, the words "estimate", "project", "belief", "anticipate", "intend", "expect", "plan", "predict", "may" or "should" and the negative of these words or such variations thereon or comparable terminology are intended to identify forward-looking statements and information. Although the Company believes, in light of the experience of their respective officers and directors, current conditions and expected future developments and other factors that have been considered appropriate, that the expectations reflected in the forward-looking statements and information in this news release are reasonable, undue reliance should not be placed on them because the parties can give no assurance that such statements will prove to be correct. Such statements and information reflect the current view of the Company. There are risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially from those contemplated in those forward-looking statements and information.

By their nature, forward-looking statements involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors which may cause our actual results, performance or achievements, or other future events, to be materially different from any future results, performance or achievements or implied by such forward-looking statements. There are a number of important factors that could cause the Company's actual results to differ materially from those indicated or implied by forward-looking statements and information. The Company undertakes no obligation to comment on analyses, expectations or statements made by third parties in respect of its securities or its financial or operating results (as applicable). data.title: India's Adani Wilmar tumbles as Adani launches discounted stake sale
data.description:
** Shares of Adani Wilmar fall 8.6% to 295.7 rupees, on track for their worst day in nearly two months, if losses hold

** Adani Group said on Thursday it plans to sell up to 20% stake in Adani Wilmar to the public

** Adani, which holds a nearly 44% stake in the joint venture with Singapore's Wilmar International , will sell up to 13.5% to the public in an offer for sale beginning Jan 10, with an option to sell an additional 6.5%

** The sale shall take place over two trading days - Jan 10 and Jan 13

** Adani Group sets floor price at 275 rupees apiece, a ~15% discount to Thursday's closing price of 323.45 rupees - LSEG data

** Group to sell stake to focus on infrastructure business, while Singapore's Wilmar International would acquire a maximum of 31.06% stake in ADAW

** Five analysts covering ADAW on avg have a "hold" rating; median PT is 385 rupees - LSEG data

** ADAW stock down 9% so far this week, on track to log its worst week in two months

data.title: Xiaomi's Core Businesses Likely Remained Resilient in 4Q of 2024 — Market Talk
data.description: Xiaomi's core businesses likely remained resilient in 4Q of 2024, OCBC analysts write in a note. The company was a "star performer" last year as share prices more than doubled on year, they say. The strong share performance was driven by solid growth in its core businesses on resilient margins and stronger-than-expected EV business margins, they say. Xiaomi's smartphone segment gross profit margin may bottom out in 4Q with potential improvement ahead given higher average selling price and lower component cost, they add. OCBC maintains its buy rating for the stock with a fair value at HK$40.50. Shares last at HK$33.60.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: China's Central Bank Suspends Bond Buying as Yields, Yuan Fall — Update
data.description:
China's central bank has hit pause on government bond purchases, a move that comes as long-term yields hit fresh lows amid expectations of more monetary policy easing.

The People's Bank of China cited excess demand for government bonds as the reason for its decision, saying the suspension was made effective this month. Buying will resume at an appropriate time, it said in a statement Friday.

"This announcement indicates that the PBOC is concerned about the recent decline of government bond yields," said Zhiwei Zhang, chief economist at Pinpoint Asset Management.

Since last year, China's bond yields have continuously touched fresh record lows as investors concerned by persistent weakness in the economy rush to haven assets. Bond prices have an inverse relationship to interest rates.

Despite the PBOC's efforts to cool the rally, traders including commercial banks and insurers have remained largely undeterred, prompting the central bank to hand out regulatory punishments in recent months.

Chinese leaders' pledge to cut rates further sparked another bull run in an already-overheated bond market. With the PBOC reiterating monetary easing pledges in recent weeks, bearish investors are betting on more stimulus to revive sluggish economic growth and fend off disinflationary pressures.

With the benchmark 10-year Chinese treasury bond yield tumbling to an all-time low of 1.6% earlier this month, the yield gap between China and the U.S. has reached an unprecedented level of 300 basis points. That's heaping pressure on a yuan battered by tariff threats from the incoming Trump administration and broad dollar strength.

Treasury yields in the U.S. have been driven up by recent strong economic data and concerns that President-elect Donald Trump's policies might boost inflation. The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note hit its highest level since April earlier this week.

Since the start of December, the yuan has weakened 1.3% against the dollar and is hovering around 16-month lows. Many economists predict the currency will end the year at around 7.5 to the dollar, the weakest in nearly two decades. There is some speculation that Chinese authorities could let the yuan to depreciate more against the greenback to help exporters in the event of more U.S. tariffs on China-made goods, which could erode exports' role as an economic growth driver.

But the central bank on Thursday announced plans to sell a record 60 billion yuan, equivalent to $8.2 billion, of six-month central bank bills in Hong Kong this month, a move that would drain offshore yuan liquidity in the global financial hub and make it harder to short the currency.

The PBOC has also been stabilizing the onshore yuan by setting a strong daily reference rate. Each morning, it publishes a reference rate for the yuan against several currencies including the dollar. The yuan is allowed to trade 2% above or below that level.

Citing BOCI economist Guan Tao, a former foreign-exchange regulator, the PBOC-affiliated Financial News said Thursday that markets should avoid over-interpreting the central bank's change to a stance of moderately loose monetary policy. The PBOC announced the change — the first in 14 years — in December.

Economists view Friday's move as another attempt to support the yuan by stabilizing yields. The effort suggests growing discomfort with declining yields and the unnerving message sent by the bond rally that the public is pessimistic about the economy.

The bond-buying halt sends a signal: the PBOC does not want to see Chinese government bond yields fall further and add more pressure on the yuan, Pinpoint's Zhang said.

Write to Singapore editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: TalkMed Appoints Independent Financial Adviser for Proposed Privatization
data.description:
TalkMed appointed KPMG Corporate Finance as independent financial adviser for the proposed privatization of the company by way of a scheme of arrangement, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

In an earlier filing, TW Troy offered to acquire all the issued and paid-up shares in the company's capital by way of a scheme of arrangement.

TW Troy would pay or procure a payment of SG$0.456 in cash per scheme share held by shareholders.

Shares of the company gained over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Imugene Receives Nearly AU$12 Million R&D Tax Refund
data.description:
Imugene received a tax refund of AU$11.7 million for the fiscal year ended June 30, 2023, from the Australian federal government's research and development tax incentive scheme, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The scheme encourages Australian firms to conduct research and development activities by providing a refund for eligible expenditures.

Funds from the rebate will be used by the immunotherapy firm to advance the development of products in the pipeline, the filing said.

The company's shares fell past 2% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: Sunac China: Considering Implications of Winding Up Petition on Company
BBVA Lowers Minimum Acceptance Threshold for Banco Sabadell Takeover Offer
data.description:
Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria said Thursday it reduced the minimum acceptance condition for its proposal to take over fellow Spanish lender Banco de Sabadell .

Instead of the original acceptance threshold of at least 50% of the target's share capital, the condition was changed to at least more than half of the voting rights in Banco Sabadell. The offeror said the revision "implies a more favorable treatment" for the recipients of the proposal.

Under the new condition, BBVA will only need the acceptance from shareholders representing at least 2,680,726,000 shares in the target, assuming no changes to Banco Sabadell's treasury shares.

data.title: Australia's Calix gains on US funding for decarbonisation projects
data.description:
** Shares of Calix rise as much as 2.68% to A$0.765, posting their biggest intraday percentage gain since Dec. 31

** Environmental tech co says two of its carbon dioxide mitigation business projects have been granted funds of nearly $3 mln by the U.S. Department of Energy

** Says the two projects are based in Virginia and Missouri

** More than 95,900 shares change hands, compared with the 30-day average volume of 311,785 shares

** Stock fell by 78.1% in 2024

data.title: See How a Fire in the Hills Turned Into a Historic Blaze — WSJ
data.description:
By Carl Churchill, Peter Champelli and Katherine Sayre

A brush fire on Tuesday set off one of the most destructive blazes in Los Angeles history: the Palisades fire. By Thursday evening, nearly 20,000 acres of land and thousands of structures had been burned, displacing thousands of residents.

Here's how the blaze swiftly traveled from the brush-filled hills to nearby residential enclaves, forcing residents to quickly move south through gridlocked paths.

A preliminary aerial survey indicated that approximately 5,316 structures might have been destroyed by the Palisades fire, the California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection said Thursday. The Palisades fire was 6% contained by Thursday evening.

Public officials are still struggling to contain the wildfires across LA — and take stock of their toll.

  • Max Rust, Andrew Mollica, Meghan Petersen, Dan Lyon and Taylor Umlauf contributed to this article.

Write to Carl Churchill at carl.churchill@wsj.com, Peter Champelli at peter.champelli@wsj.com and Katherine Sayre at katherine.sayre@wsj.com

data.title: Logan Group: Reached Agreement on Restructuring Proposal
Althea Group Unit Enters Amended Agreement with US-Based Beer Company's Cannabis Unit
data.description:
Althea Group Holdings said its Peak Processing Solutions unit signed an amended multi-year manufacturing agreement with BBCCC, the unit of Boston Beer Company, for the production of their cannabis beverage portfolio in Canada, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Under the updated terms, Peak will reserve capacity for Boston Beer at its facility, starting with a minimum of over 800,000 units for 2025, the filing said.

The agreement, effective from Jan. 1 through to Aug. 31, 2026, features automatic three-year renewals unless terminated with 180 days' notice, the company said.

The amendment boosts Peak's cash flow and ensures a steady supply for Boston Beer's TeaPot and Emerald Hour cannabis cocktail brands, the filing added.

data.title: Logan Group Says It Entered Into Holistic Creditor Support Agreement With Ad Hoc Group
data.description:
Logan Group Co Ltd :

  • ENTRY INTO HOLISTIC CREDITOR SUPPORT AGREEMENT- WITH AD HOC GROUP

  • REACHED AN AGREEMENT ON CREDITOR SUPPORT AGREEMENT DATED 10 JANUARY IN RELATION TO HOLISTIC RESTRUCTURING PROPOSAL

  • TO PAY EARLY-BIRD CSA FEE TO EACH CONSENTING CREDITOR EQUAL TO 0.125% OF AGGREGATE PRINCIPAL AMOUNT OF OFFSHORE DEBT HELD

data.title: Aarti Drugs to Acquire 26% Stake in Renewable Energy Company
data.description:
Aarti Drugs has agreed to acquire a 26% stake in renewable energy company Pro-Zeal Green Power Six from Prozeal Green Power for 16.5 million Indian rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the stock exchanges.

Pro-Zeal Green Power Six is a special-purpose vehicle formed to develop, operate, and maintain a solar power plant for captive consumption by the company.

Under the pact, Aarti Drugs will invest 630,000 rupees through subscription to 63,000 equity shares of 10 rupees each of Pro-Zeal Green Power Six. It will also invest 15.9 million rupees by way of compulsory convertible debentures in a phased manner.

The transaction is expected to be completed in 30 days.

data.title: Apollo considering $9.5 bln investment in Seven & i buyout, Bloomberg says
data.description:
Apollo Global Management is considering investing as much as 1.5 trillion yen ($9.5 billion) in a management buyout of Japan's Seven & i Holdings , Bloomberg News said on Friday, citing several people with knowledge of the matter.

Under the plan, Seven & i's founding family will invest 500 billion yen in equity, trading company Itochu Corp will invest over 1 trillion yen in equity, and Apollo up to 1.5 trillion yen in preferred shares, according to the report.

In an interview with Reuters, Apollo declined to comment on whether it is engaged with parties for a Seven & i deal.

($1 = 158.2300 yen)

data.title: New Zealand's Construction Cost Growth Eases in Q4 2024; Pick-Up Seen in 2025
data.description:
Growth in New Zealand's construction cost eased in the final quarter of 2024, but the sector may see improving conditions in the new year amid cheaper mortgage rates, according to a Friday report by CoreLogic.

The cost to build a "standard" single-story, three-bedroom, two-bathroom standalone dwelling in New Zealand rose 0.6% in the three months to December, below the 1.1% increase in the third quarter and the long-term average quarterly rise of 1%.

Annual growth over the past 12 months also eased to 1.1% from 2.4% in 2023, based on CoreLogic's Cordell Construction Cost Index.

CoreLogic chief property economist Kelvin Davidson said the latest numbers offer little surprise since the supply of materials is no longer an issue and given the slowdown in new dwellings consented.

"As a result, there's been reduced pressure on the industry's capacity, which naturally dampens cost growth, both for materials and labor," Davidson said.

Looking ahead, construction conditions are expected to improve in 2025 on the back of a decline in mortgage rates.

"There are also signs in the new dwellings data from Stats NZ that a floor may have been reached and that a rise in construction is likely in 2025, Davidson added.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Thursday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
U.K. Pound, Bonds Slide; U.S. Stock Market Closed

Volatility in U.K. asset prices evokes memories of the 2022 market meltdown.

China's Central Bank Suspends Purchase of Treasury Bonds in Open Markets

The move comes after the country's long-term yields hit fresh lows in recent weeks on policy easing hopes.

The World's Bond Markets Are Uneasy. It's the U.S.'s Fault.

The yield on 10-year U.K. government debt rose to the highest since 2008, the latest casualty of a global rout spurred by rising yields on Treasury notes.

The Stock Market Is Looking for Its Next Boost. Earnings Won't Provide.

Companies across the board are less likely to surpass expectations, while stocks have already taken the past few years to reflect growing earnings and a growing economy.

Investors are on edge as Friday's jobs report could make or break the stock-market rally

Rising Treasury yields are ratcheting up the anxiety for equity investors. Thursday's stock-market closure offered only a brief reprieve from the mounting tension.

L.A. wildfires have caused more than $135 billion in economic losses - and counting

Fires across Southern California are already one of the most damaging in the states history.

Why two Fed officials doubt the need for many more rate cuts

One official warned that "progress on inflation could continue to stall."

Deficit Fears Are Driving Up Bond Yields. Stocks Could Take a Hit.

Rising Treasury yields are a risk for the stock market, strategists say.

Bank of England Monitoring Gilt Market, But Sees Moves as Orderly, Says Breeden

"So far the moves have been orderly," Breeden said after a speech in Scotland. "So far so good."

U.S. Mortgage Rates Climb to Highest Since July

U.S. mortgage rates are back at their highest level since last summer, frustrating home buyers waiting for a break from some of the steepest borrowing costs in more than a decade.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 11 PM ET
data.description:
The Pacific Palisades Inferno Won't Engulf the Insurance Industry

The insurance industry has been thriving as climate change increases the demand for coverage.

Fox Must Face Smartmatic's Defamation Lawsuit, Appeals Court Rules

The voting-machine company seeks billions in damages.

BlackRock Withdraws From Climate Coalition, Backpedaling Again on ESG

The world's largest asset manager is latest Wall Street firm to quit climate groups.

Elon Musk's xAI Launches Stand-Alone Grok App

The AI startup launched its first stand-alone consumer app, as the company tries to catch up with more established players such as OpenAI and Google in the generative AI race.

Delta Air Lines' Earnings Can Make or Break the Sector's Rally. 1 Metric Is Key.

Airline stocks have been flying recently, which makes earnings season tricky to navigate for investors.

Southwest Airlines CFO Tammy Romo to Retire as Airline Embarks on Turnaround

Romo is retiring after more than three decades with the carrier as it works on a turnaround after lagging behind some of its peers in recent years.

Wells Fargo Taps JPMorgan Executive to Lead Its Credit-Card Business

Growth in credit cards has been a focus for CEO Charlie Scharf.

DHL Buys Into the Growing Retail Returns Business

The acquisition of a returns services provider comes as retailers are grappling with the higher volumes that shoppers are sending back.

KKR, PSP Investments Partner to Buy Stake in Two American Electric Power Companies

KKR is partnering with the Public Sector Pension Investment Board to buy a 19.9% interest in two units of American Electric Power for $2.82 billion.

Airbus Comes Slightly Short of Plane Delivery Target Amid Supply-Chain Bottlenecks

The European plane maker delivered fewer aircraft than expected last year as persisting supply-chain hurdles weighed on production.

data.title: India's TCS jumps 4% on signs of demand revival
data.description:
Shares of India's No. 1 software services exporter Tata Consultancy Services jumped 4% in early trade on Friday, set for their best day since late-November, as the company signalled it was seeing signs of a demand revival.

TCS CEO K Krithivasan told reporters in a post-earnings press conference on Thursday that the company was seeing more confidence in discretionary programmes as clients were taking less time to sign deals.

Krithivasan's comments overshadowed a weak performance in North American revenue for the fifth straight quarter.

TCS shares were last up 4.2% at 4,206.30 rupees. It led a 1.9% jump in IT stocks and was the top gainer on the blue-chip Nifty 50 index.

CLSA upgraded TCS to "outperform" from "hold" citing Kritivasan's comments and forecast a 14% earnings per share growth in fiscal year 2026, compared to the 8% average it expects for 2025.

In a call with analysts, Kritivasan pointed to a recovery in demand in most sectors that it serves, including banking, financial services and insurance (BFSI) and consumer – which accounted for about 46% of third-quarter revenue.

The improved deal execution and demand commentary are signs that point to an upcycle, Bernstein analysts said.

($1 = 85.8600 Indian rupees)

data.title: IDBI Capital initiates coverage on Patel Engineering with a buy rating; ups target price by 54%
data.description:
IDBI Capital has initiated coverage on Patel Engineering Ltd with a "Buy" rating and a target price of Rs 76 per share, representing a 54% upside from the current market price.

The target price reflects expectations of continued strong order inflows, particularly from the hydro power segment, along with the company’s strategic diversification across both geographic and sectoral lines. These factors are anticipated to drive the company’s revenue growth, as per the brokerage.

Founded in 1949, Patel Engineering (PEC) specialises in complex civil infrastructure projects, including hydro power plants, tunneling, urban infrastructure, and irrigation. The company has navigated multiple capital expenditure cycles in India and currently holds an order book worth Rs 191 billion, which is four times its trailing twelve-month revenue.

Follow our market blog to catch more

Since COVID-19, PEC has experienced a significant upturn in order inflows. In FY23, it recorded its highest-ever annual order inflow of Rs 78 billion. As a major beneficiary of India’s infrastructure capital expenditure in the hydro power sector, PEC anticipates bidding opportunities for over 30GW in the coming years. The company has posted a consistent double-digit revenue CAGR of 18% from FY20 to FY23, with its YTD FY24 revenue increasing by 20% year-on-year.

PEC’s leverage has declined to 0.6x in 9MFY24 from around 1x pre-COVID, supported by internal accruals, non-core asset sales, and equity raises over the past four years, it added.

IDBI Capital said the company’s robust construction capabilities, developed through years of executing highly technical projects, are expected to play a key role in its growth. Operating margins are forecasted to remain stable, driven by a strong order book in the hydro segment. Additionally, PEC’s low debt-to-equity ratio and improving return ratios highlight its strong financial position.

At the current market price, the stock is trading at a valuation of 16x FY25E P/E, 13.5x FY26E P/E, and 11.5x FY27E P/E. Using a sum-of-the-parts (SOTP) valuation methodology, IDBI Capital has assigned a P/E of 12x FY27E for the core construction business, resulting in a valuation of Rs 63 per share. Additionally, the value of land assets, other investments, and recoveries from arbitration claims is estimated at Rs 20 per share. After applying a 65% valuation factor to these investments, this translates to Rs 14 per share, IDBI added.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Dahao Tech's Integrated Circuit Unit Renews Certification
data.description:
Beijing Dahao Technology's subsidiary, Beijing Xinghan Internet, was re-certified in Beijing Certification Institutions' list of integrated circuit enterprises, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The renewed certification is valid for three years, the Chinese sewing machine maker said.

Companies with the certification can pay a corporate income tax at a rate of 15%.

The company's shares rose more than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Iron ore set for weekly loss on soft demand, but China stimulus limits fall
data.description:
Iron ore futures edged up on Friday, aided by stimulus expansion from top-consumer China, but were poised to end the week lower on sluggish Chinese consumption and softening demand for the steelmaking ingredient.

The most-traded May iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) ended morning trade 0.2% higher at 752 yuan ($102.57) a metric ton, declining 2.15% this week.

The benchmark February iron ore (SZZFG5) on the Singapore Exchange eased 0.16% to $96.9 a ton at 0357 GMT, falling 1.17% this week.

Seasonal demand for steel has declined and the demand for steel raw materials is similarly low, Chinese consultancy Galaxy Futures said.

Steel sales volume expectations have fallen dramatically, said Chinese consultancy Mysteel, quoting a report from China's National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC).

"While a reduction in steel supply is expected this month as more steelmakers observe maintenance stoppages, the NDRC emphasised that this is unlikely to be enough to counterbalance the shrinking demand."

Still, demand from winter stockpiling is expected to provide some support to prices and the market is awaiting potential support from policy initiatives, Mysteel added.

Earlier this week, Beijing expanded its consumer trade-in scheme in an effort to revive demand in the sluggish household sector.

Growth in China was estimated at 4.9% for 2024 and projected to be 4.8% this year, partly offset by subdued consumption growth and lingering property sector weakness.

Meanwhile, markets should avoid over-interpreting Beijing's "moderately loose" monetary policy, Financial News, a publication backed by China's central bank, said, citing economist Guan Tao.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE declined, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) down 1.05% and 0.59%, respectively.

Most steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange rose. Rebar closed 0.12% higher, hot-rolled coil climbed 0.15% and stainless steel gained 0.19%. Wire rod (SWRcv1) dipped 0.14%.

($1 = 7.3319 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Market Chatter: Nvidia CEO Confirms Samsung as Key Supplier for GeForce RTX 50 Series GPUs
data.description:
Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, said Wednesday that Samsung Electronics is a key supplier of GDDR7 memory for the GeForce RTX 50 series GPUs, alongside other partners, Pulse News reported Thursday.

His statement follows previous remarks at CES 2025 that only mentioned Micron Technology, sparking speculation about exclusivity. Huang emphasized the inclusion of Samsung's contributions, addressing concerns raised by the omission, the report said.

Shares of Samsung Electronics fell nearly 1% in recent trade on Friday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Qatar Trade Surplus Narrows Slightly
data.description:
The trade surplus in Qatar narrowed a little in November 2024, standing at QAR 16.67 billion, compared to QAR 16.73 billion in the same month last year, as imports rose more than exports.

Year-on-year, exports grew 1.3% to a one-year low of QAR 26.87 billion, while imports increased 4% to QAR 10.2 billion.

For the first eleven months of the year, the country’s trade surplus shrank to QAR 197.21 billion from EUR 222.67 billion in the corresponding period of the previous year.

data.title: Toymakers build fun Hong Kong stocks a playground
data.description:
Bloks shares surged on their debut on overwhelming demand. The $2 bln firm, which specialises in Transformers and other figurines, is tapping into China's collectible toy frenzy led by peer Pop Mart. The duo spotlight a cheap and cheerful part of an otherwise gloomy economy.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @mak_robyn on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Shares of Chinese toymaker Bloks rose 82% from their initial public offering price in early trading on their Hong Kong debut on Jan. 10.

The company raised HK$1.67 billion ($215 million) after pricing at the top of the marketed range and increasing the amount of shares sold. Local retail investors bid 6,000 times the amount of shares on offer.

data.title: DEADLINE REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Investigates Claims on Behalf of Investors of Cassava
data.description:
Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson Encourages Investors Who Suffered Losses Exceeding $75,000 In Cassava To Contact Him Directly To Discuss Their Options

If you suffered losses exceeding $75,000 in Cassava between February 7, 2024 and November 24, 2024 and would like to discuss your legal rights, call Faruqi & Faruqi partner Josh Wilson directly at 877-247-4292 or 212-983-9330 (Ext. 1310).

[You may also click here for additional information]

New York, New York--(Newsfile Corp. - January 9, 2025) - Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP, a leading national securities law firm, is investigating potential claims against Cassava Sciences, Inc. ("Cassava" or the "Company") and reminds investors of the February 10, 2025 deadline to seek the role of lead plaintiff in a federal securities class action that has been filed against the Company.

Faruqi & Faruqi is a leading national securities law firm with offices in New York, Pennsylvania, California and Georgia. The firm has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors since its founding in 1995. See www.faruqilaw.com.

The complaint alleges that defendants provided investors with material information concerning Cassava's leading drug candidate, simufilam. Defendants' statements included, among other things, clear confidence in simufilam's ability to treat Alzheimer's Disease. Defendants provided these overwhelmingly positive statements to investors while, at the same time, disseminating materially false and misleading statements and/or concealing material adverse facts concerning the true capabilities of Cassava's drugs; notably, that Company simply did not have a drug that was capable of abating the progression of Alzheimer's Disease. Such statements absent these material facts caused Plaintiff and other shareholders to purchase Cassava's securities at artificially inflated prices. On November 25, 2024, Cassava released topline results for the first of its two ongoing Phase 3 studies on simufilam, the "ReThink-ALZ" study. The results indicated that simufilam failed to meet each of the pre-specified primary, secondary, and exploratory endpoints; in sum, simufilam failed to outperform the placebo.

Following this news, the price of Cassava's common stock declined dramatically. From a closing market price of $26.48 per share on November 22, 2024, Cassava's stock price fell to $4.30 per share on November 25, 2024, a decline of about 83.76% in the span of just a single day.

The court-appointed lead plaintiff is the investor with the largest financial interest in the relief sought by the class who is adequate and typical of class members who directs and oversees the litigation on behalf of the putative class. Any member of the putative class may move the Court to serve as lead plaintiff through counsel of their choice, or may choose to do nothing and remain an absent class member. Your ability to share in any recovery is not affected by the decision to serve as a lead plaintiff or not.

Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP also encourages anyone with information regarding Cassava's conduct to contact the firm, including whistleblowers, former employees, shareholders and others.

To learn more about the Cassava Sciences, Inc. class action, go to www.faruqilaw.com/SAVA or call Faruqi & Faruqi partner Josh Wilson directly at 877-247-4292 or 212-983-9330 (Ext. 1310).

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn, on X, or on Facebook.

Attorney Advertising. The law firm responsible for this advertisement is Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP (www.faruqilaw.com). Prior results do not guarantee or predict a similar outcome with respect to any future matter. We welcome the opportunity to discuss your particular case. All communications will be treated in a confidential manner.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/236496
